metadata.title: Ouroboros Thesis v3 — The Lutar Invariant: A Nine-Axis Closed-Form Scalar Law for Runtime Trust in Agentic AI Systems
metadata.description:
v3 of the Ouroboros Thesis introduces the Lutar Invariant Λ, a closed-form scalar in [0, 1] that aggregates nine independent runtime-trust axes into a single auditable number. We prove uniqueness under four axioms (monotonicity, zero-pinning, Egyptian inspectability, Page-curve concavity). The third axiom is novel: it requires the weight set to be representable as a finite sum of distinct unit fractions in the sense of the Rhind Mathematical Papyrus 2/n table (c. 1650 BCE), which makes the law bit-exact reproducible across heterogeneous runtimes. v3 extends v1 (cleanliness, position paper) and v2 (empirical companion) to the full nine-axis envelope: Cleanliness, Horizon (Page-curve bounded reversibility), Resonance (Tesla-era impedance / Kuramoto coherence), Frustum (three-witness Jaccard reconciliation, MMP-14), Geometry (Gauss / Aristotle), Invariance (Lorentz covariance), Moral (dual-use review), Being (Plato-Socrates elenchus), and Non-measurability (Jamneshan-Shalom-Tao 2026).

An open-source reference implementation accompanies the paper: 150 declared Vitest tests in the single @szl-holdings/ouroboros package, runtime release v6.1.0 (commit e9fc4b86eae18bb7401b14cb0e53900ba8e47ad8). Earlier drafts of this metadata claimed 1,372 tests across 24 packages with a 925 TypeScript / 447 Python split; that surface was roadmap and is retracted.

This is the third in the Ouroboros series. Predecessor DOIs:

  • v1 position paper: 10.5281/zenodo.19867281
  • v2 empirical companion: 10.5281/zenodo.19934129

't Hooft, G. (1993). Dimensional reduction in quantum gravity. arXiv:gr-qc/9310026

Pozar, D. (2011). Microwave Engineering, 4th ed. Wiley
Cobb, C. W. & Douglas, P. H. (1928). A theory of production. American Economic Review 18(1), 139
Yager, R. R. (1988). On ordered weighted averaging aggregation operators. IEEE Trans. SMC 18, 183

Almheiri, A., Marolf, D., Polchinski, J., Sully, J. (2013). Black holes: complementarity or firewalls? JHEP 02, 062. arXiv:1207.3123
Miyato, T., Loewe, S., Geiger, A., Welling, M. (2025). Artificial Kuramoto Oscillatory Neurons. ICLR 2025. arXiv:2410.13821
NIST AI Risk Management Framework 1.0 (2023)
DoD Responsible AI Strategy (2022)
ISO/IEC 42001:2023 — AI Management Systems
Rhind Mathematical Papyrus (c. 1650 BCE)
Moscow Mathematical Papyrus, problem 14 (c. 1850 BCE)
Liu Hui, Nine Chapters on the Mathematical Art commentary (c. 250 CE)

Lutar, S. P. (2026). Ouroboros v1: position paper. Zenodo. DOI 10.5281/zenodo.19867281
Lutar, S. P. (2026). Ouroboros v2: empirical companion. Zenodo. DOI 10.5281/zenodo.19934129
metadata.notes: Open-source reference implementation: github.com/szl-holdings/ouroboros (v6.1.0). Test surface: 150 declared Vitest tests in the single @szl-holdings/ouroboros package, 9 Lambda axes. Compounds DOI 10.5281/zenodo.19867281 (v1) and DOI 10.5281/zenodo.19934129 (v2).
